ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Shish Tawook Wrap; FOODON_00002473 (chicken wrap) is the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for shish tawook wrap; A0FKP (grilled chicken meat) is used as the closest parent anchor for the dominant protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for shish tawook wrap as a composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One shish tawook wrap weighing approximately 300 g, consisting of grilled marinated chicken, flatbread, garlic sauce, vegetables, and pickles.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken shawarma wrap / grilled chicken wrap — composite estimation from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries: Chicken breast, grilled (FDC ID 171477); Pita bread, white (FDC ID 175023); Garlic sauce / toum (FDC ID 172231 olive oil base); Tomato raw (FDC ID 170457); Lettuce raw (FDC ID 169247)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for shish tawook wrap exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-ingredient composition: approximately 40% grilled chicken breast, 30% flatbread (lavash/pita), 15% garlic sauce (toum, olive-oil and garlic base), 10% tomato and lettuce, 5% pickles. Per-100g values derived from this blend. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — pita bread and mixed chicken wrap analogues; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for shish tawook wrap. GI estimated at 52 based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle (pita/lavash flatbread, GI ~57 per University of Sydney GI Database) blended with the protein and fat content of the wrap, which lowers the effective GI. Mixed chicken-and-bread wrap analogues in the Sydney GI Database cluster in the 48–56 range. GL per serving = (52 × 55.5 g carbohydrate per 300 g serving) / 100 = 28.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 420 mg per 100 g; a full 300 g wrap delivers approximately 1260 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit shish tawook wrap consumption to half a wrap or request reduced-salt preparation, as the garlic sauce, pickles, and marinade collectively contribute substantial sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~52) and high glycemic load per full serving (~28.9 g GL) due to the flatbread carbohydrate base; protein and fat content partially attenuate postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a half-wrap portion, choose wholemeal flatbread where available, and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to moderate the glycemic load to below 20 per meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat is relatively low at 1.6 g per 100 g; however, the high sodium content and caloric density of the garlic sauce (toum) are relevant cardiovascular risk factors.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should request reduced garlic sauce and avoid pickles to lower sodium intake, and limit portion to one wrap per meal given the overall energy density of approximately 585 kcal per full serving.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Shish Tawook Wrap is a popular Turkish and Levantine grilled chicken wrap consisting of marinated chicken pieces (shish tawook) served inside a flatbread such as lavash or pita. The chicken is marinated in a mixture of yogurt, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, and spices including paprika and cumin, then grilled over charcoal or a gas grill until tender and slightly charred. The wrap is assembled with garlic sauce (toum), fresh tomato, lettuce, and pickled vegetables, then rolled tightly. The result is a satisfying, protein-rich meal with a smoky, tangy, and garlicky flavour profile and a soft yet slightly chewy texture from the flatbread. In Singapore, shish tawook wraps are served at Turkish and Lebanese restaurants such as Sultan Palace at Boat Quay, typically priced around SGD 17.90. Nutritionally, a 300 g wrap provides approximately 585 kcal, 40.5 g protein, 23.4 g fat, and 55.5 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is high (~1260 mg per wrap) due to the marinade, garlic sauce, and pickles, making it a moderate concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low-medium boundary), but the glycemic load per full serving is approximately 28.9, which is clinically significant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The dish is halal-certified in most Turkish and Lebanese restaurant contexts in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a shish tawook wrap?
What is in a Turkish chicken wrap?
Is shish tawook wrap good for diabetics?
How much sodium in a shish tawook wrap?
What is the difference between shish tawook and shawarma?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for shish tawook wrap exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-ingredient composition using USDA SR Legacy data for individual components. GI is estimated by analogy with pita-based chicken wraps from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source data for this composite dish. All other -1 values in per_100g have been replaced with estimated values from USDA SR Legacy component data. Serving size of 300 g is a reasonable estimate for a restaurant-style shish tawook wrap as served at Sultan Palace, Boat Quay, Singapore (field survey listing price SGD 17.90). The food_id GD000284 is retained from the field survey data seed record. This dish is not present in the HPB Singapore FD-series food index. Multilingual names are provided for Turkish cuisine served in Singapore context: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are included per Singaporean multilingual naming rules. The romanized Turkish name 'Shish Tavuk Dürüm' is included as a romanized alias. Ontology anchors use closest_parent match types as no exact composite-dish codes exist in FoodOn or FoodEx2 for this specific preparation.
